author: @AmaterasuSolar
text:
Wish I was not elderly, disabled, denied assistance lost everything, destitute and technically homeless.  Would love to support Your work!  My dad worked with T.Townsend Brown on electrogravitics and so I know We have tech to create both gravity control and free energy.  My work is to end accounting for Our energy added into a system (money in all forms, from trade/barter to electronic bits) such that We ALL can live as richly as We choose on Our planet.

Humbly, I hope You check out what I offer.

author: @sergeikurek
text: Sorry, Robert. First time after almost 5 years watchin i hit "Dislike". Thanks for all good ideas you shared for many years. But for me "Members only" philosophy  is a boat which I hope is going to sink.

replies.author: @johnmcfadden9336
replies.text:
Sergei Kurek 
If you look at the content of some of robs videos they are ground breaking and yet he gets very little appreciation for the gems he freely shares,  look at the likes compared to views and the ratio is pitiful  so I don’t suppose many people donate either 
To realise what rob has achieved would have taken a vast investment  in time and money, if he is to realise his full potential and the sooner the better for everyone he could really do with some real support 
The fact he has taken this step is a very poor reflection on us collectively and high lights our selfish gimme gimme nature 
God bless this little boat ( graphene reinforced hull ) and all who sail in her

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: I. understand mate - but John makes the point very well. The information isn't free - someone somewhere has to pay for it and all this time it has been me - you don't think sharing the load even a little bit is appropriate that's ok - but there is a consequence to that - the folks here are a mixed bag and some folks actively support what I do some passively support and others just free load on the good will of many. In so many ways it is just not fair to free load. I am hoping this will do something about that and make a return to those who have supported me over the years but not give anything to those who just want to take advantage. Having said that - I don't take your dislike negatively mate - it's a complex issue and there is no clear view - we all do what we think is best and I am happy to say it may not be

replies.author: @Killianwsh
replies.text:
@john mcfadden: Well stated Sir!  I believe your  100% on point in you estimation and thinking regarding this!   I personally think it's  quite telling that some of Robs viewers obviously have the brain power to appreciate the enormity of what he has already accomplished and shared on this channel and yet some how cannot appreciate the time, effort, and expense he and the FWG team have had to put in (for YEARS prior to ever being able to post the first video!) enough to remember to "thumbs up" "subscribe" and or even to say " Thank you!" 
Its disgraceful!   No one raised with any sense of manners would fail to at least say "thank you" if they accepted even a glass of water graciously offered for free!  (particularly so if they were thirsty and had none!)  Yet THOUSANDS have watched and benefited from Rob's efforts and generosity and haven't even paused to click the "like" button!
I want to thank you for your direct and immediate reproach of this  individual  on Rob's behalf!  I must  commend you for also doing so in a composed, articulate, and polite manner.  I rather doubt I would have been able to show such restraint in the face of such egregious  self entitlement , admonishing Rob for asking for PENNIES  in exchange for his knowledge instead of thanklessly giving it all for free, as if Rob owed him some life debt!  Despicable!
Apologies for the length of this comment,  I feel very strongly about this subj. ,as I, having broken my spine a couple years ago, am currently forced to live by the grace and generosity of  my  family while I learn to adapt to being "differently-abled" & find a new vocation accordingly.  Lacking  the resources and physical capacity to simply "go back to school"  I've had to seek out those on the internet gracious enough to share their knowledge so I can learn from them.  I found Rob's channel in the course of that journey and am eternally grateful to him for his generosity.  Even though I cannot, without adding to the financial burden already shouldered by my family, yet afford even the pennies he's asked for his membership (which he is more than due!), I've made it a point to at  least thank him in the comments , "like", and refer others to assist his channel until I can do more!  
It is very good to know there are others out there who are similarly appreciative of all Rob and his team have already given, AND have enough stone & integrity to soundly put ingrates in their place on his behalf.
Much Respect.
Killian

replies.author: @Killianwsh
replies.text:
@Sergei Kurek:  Superficially "thanking" someone for the free lunch you've happily enjoyed at his table "for five years"  while admonishing him for asking for only PENNIES for access to the "dessert" is no thanks at all!   Compounding that, by openly expressing your hope that his MORE than reasonable endeavor FAIL, is beyond EXCEPTIONALLY RUDE!  It broadcasts the self-entitled current essence of your character and a parasitic view of the world!
In the belief that anyone, if they choose too, can learn, grow, and change, here's a couple facts that may prevent you from colliding with the reality walls of life and particularly on this subject.
1. The world owes you NOTHING.
2. Rob owes you NOTHING.
3. Rob's team owes you nothing!
4. Rob and his team have already, over the past 5 years, graciously 
 shared enough information (often in KINDERGARTEN LEVEL DETAIL that even a 6yr old can follow) that almost anyone can utilize it to enhance their lives and those of their family in MANY different ways should they choose too. 
5. There THOUSANDS of colleges and universities around the world that will happily teach you the foundations of the knowledge that Rob and his team have PAID FOR ALREADY OUT OF THEIR OWN POCKETS and taken a good portion of THEIR LIVES to learn.  I assure you...you will NEVER! EVER! be able to purchase the combined information they posses for $5/ month!!  (let alone the genius of knowledge to put that information to use!)  
6. Complaining that you feel "excluded" over a membership price lower than a burger and frys at the crappiest ar$e hole burger stand only makes you a laughing stock to those who measure value by exclusivity because almost EVERYONE can likely afford it!  And makes you look like a MORON to EVERYONE who can comprehend the concept of exchange of VALUE!  (If you are not familiar with the last one, imagine what you'd think of someone that  complained that FT KNOX was selling 1 oz gold US 1 Dollar  coins for 50 cents instead of just giving them away, so they didn't get any and "hoped that their boat sank"!
and finally 7.   Rob can, if he chooses, boot or block you from his ch. and its content (past, present,  and future) AT ANY TIME & FOR ANY REASON!   You should not only be thankful he's kind enough not to have done so already, but should humbly apologize for being rude, lest he change his mind! ;)

If you disagree with any of the above statements, that's ok!  They are placed here in hopes they will aid you in life. Your welcome to learn from them,, ignore them, attempt to prove them incorrect, or pound sand over them. Your choice.
Have a nice day & good luck too you! :)

author: @Ivke2006
text: I think it’s a good idea to have a membership channel to generate some cash. However, make sure that non members have an idea what they miss. Also how many vids are you planning to make per month? and did you consider Patreon?

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: I did consider patron mate but left it as youtube started this and it was an easy switch. I am guaranteeing 8 per month - which is two a week but that's an a average and I can be quite prolific at times lol. I am not sure how to let folks know what they are missing without being mean - but I will mull that over for sure - cheers mate

author: @kennybentley1161
text:
you know this is kind of an interesting idea. for example, if you were to stream your work on twitch, it's common for people to "subscribe" to the channel to show their support good idea. I don't really know how you would do streaming sort of things (ie. real time), but that could be an interesting format. I certainly would want to watch sometihng like that.


I think the reason why you're not selling as much of your ink as you would imagine is the same reason why other bulk materials aren't flying off the shelf. I think people are way more interested in being a consumer than a creator (that is, purchasing a product).


there's an interesting sort of thing happening for people who stream games on twitch. it's a really interesting phenomenon that I've taken quite a liking to myself because I find the phenomenon so interesting. to get good enough to compete in the top ranks of these games takes quite a bit of skill now that esports has become a real thing. so, there's usually a pretty large amount of practice that one would need to do to get really good (something like becoming a footballer or something). so, much like professional sports, no one wants to actually take the time to become competitive at it, it's simply much easier to *watch* the streamer play the game.


when I watch a person playing a game live, I get a sense that I am somehow participating in the act of playing the game (I know, it doesn't make much sense) and the streamer uses words like "we" and "our" instead to promote this sense of participation. making cool thnigs "together" like that could be interesting thing to participate in.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: I think you are right mate and the membership idea is exactly that kind of thing, I think. Instead of selling ink to move things along we are selling the experience - so 'if you like, the product is the experience of us moving along and the experience bought is the vacarious living of being there as things are created - nice analysis mate
